evidence: call_wall_acceptance_long produced only 2 VALID_0DTE trades but contributed $640.5, $290.3/hour, and the global worst MAE at -655.2. The worst trade had early favorable excursion but a very wide target and long recovery path.
name: Call-Wall Early Realization Containment
risk: The profile may require wide targets to pay for rare convex call-wall moves; capping TP can improve MAE and exposure while suppressing total net profit.
trigger: VALID_0DTE call-wall acceptance with positive forward DEX and strike acceptance, but wide selected target distance and extended ES-VWAP state.
validation_metric: call_wall_acceptance_long worst MAE improves materially, total worst MAE moves toward <= -513.4, slow >=12h stays 0, total net profit remains >=98% of run_044, and portfolio PnL/exposure stays flat or better.

evidence: The analyzer repeatedly shows static config cannot distinguish delayed convex recoveries from clean fast winners as well as intratrade path features: first profit timing, half-TP touch, and MFE-before-MAE separate tail behavior.
name: Call-Wall Convex Recovery Monitor
risk: Path-aware logic can overfit a two-trade profile unless validated against protected fast winners and out-of-sample days.
trigger: call_wall_acceptance_long enters with wide target and extended ES-VWAP but fails to make early half-target progress or prints adverse expansion before meaningful continuation.
validation_metric: Reduce call_wall_acceptance_long worst MAE while retaining at least 70% of its PnL, keeping total score flat or better, and preserving protected fast-winner retention >=95%.
